title: Choosing a Rate-Limiting Algorithm
description: A teammate is implementing rate limiting. Help them weigh token bucket, leaky bucket, and sliding window.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I'm adding rate limiting to the public API. Token bucket or leaky bucket?

templates.text: Depends on your traffic shape. Do you want to allow short bursts or smooth them out?
templates.hint_zh: 看流量型態。你想允許短時突發,還是把它平滑掉?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Bursts are fine, as long as we don't blow up downstream.

templates.text: Then token bucket fits. It refills at a steady rate but lets clients spend a stash up front.
templates.hint_zh: 那 token bucket 比較合。它定速補充,但允許客戶端先把存量花掉。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What about sliding window counter? I keep seeing it on system design notes.

templates.text: It's a compromise. More accurate than fixed window, way cheaper than a sliding log.
templates.hint_zh: 是折衷方案。比固定窗口準確,又比 sliding log 便宜很多。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: We're running this across three nodes. Any gotchas?

templates.text: Yeah, race conditions on the counter. Easiest fix is a Redis Lua script for atomic updates.
templates.hint_zh: 對,計數器會有 race condition。最簡單就是用 Redis Lua script 做原子更新。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: And where does it live in the request path?

templates.text: Ideally at the API gateway, so backend services don't even see the dropped traffic.
templates.hint_zh: 最好放在 API gateway,讓後端服務根本看不到被擋掉的流量。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great. I'll start with token bucket and put the metrics in from day one.

templates.text: Smart. Tuning without dashboards is just guessing.
templates.hint_zh: 聰明。沒儀表板就調參數等於亂猜。
